title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Kenneth Marshall's departure, Colonel Weston, Inspector Cargraves, and Hercule Poirot discuss the suspect's behavior. Cargraves observes that Marshall’s calm and collected demeanor might cause a jury to distrust him, as he shows no outward emotion regarding his wife's murder. Poirot suggests that Marshall is "clammed up" and believes the husband is withholding information, noting that Marshall appeared suspiciously expressionless after Poirot saw Mrs. Marshall and Patrick Redfern together at Sunlight Cliff the previous night.

The group evaluates Marshall's alibi involving the use of a typewriter, noting its validity depends on whether a maid in the adjacent room heard the device. Chief Inspector Kogorov proposes that the killer is either a hotel guest or an outsider; while Marshall is a primary suspect due to potential motives of jealousy and money, Kogorov notes that Elena Marshall likely had female enemies. However, Poirot points out that the coroner, Nisden, determined the cause of death was strangulation by a man with large, strong hands, which complicates the theory of a female killer. Inspector Cargot suggests the possibility of a jealous former lover, referred to as "X," who may have followed Elena to the island by boat.

Weston reviews the hotel register to identify potential suspects. He eliminates Major Corn and his family, as they were on a sea trip that morning. Poirot provides descriptions of other guests, including the Gardners, a middle-aged couple from New York.
